hypothesis: Commodity trend target exits may still contain continuation alpha, but the prior post-target re-entry path was inert. Extending only `trend_long | Commodities` targets before exit may capture that continuation without changing entries, allocation, LLM/news, add-ons, scarce-slot routing, or other exits.
why_tested: This follows the explicit next-valid retry from exp-20260428-002: test an exit-before-reentry semantic instead of another same-ticker re-entry signal. LLM soft ranking remains sample-limited, so this deterministic exit alpha was tested instead.

parameters.single_causal_variable: trend_long Commodities target width above current 7 ATR

market_regime_summary.mid_weak: rotation-heavy bull where strategy makes money but lags indexes
market_regime_summary.old_thin: mixed-to-weak older tape with lower win rate

delta_metrics.gate4_basis: Rejected for production promotion. Although 8 ATR improved two windows and late_strong Sharpe by +0.16, it damaged mid_weak EV by -0.1035, Sharpe by -0.25, PnL by -$2,070.56, and win rate by -4.55 pp. The aggregate PnL gain was only $656.83, and wider 9/10 ATR variants materially damaged late_strong.

llm_metrics.blocker_relation: LLM soft ranking remains sample-limited, so this deterministic exit alpha was tested instead.

rejection_reason: The continuation exists in late/old, but a uniform target extension above 7 ATR is not robust enough because it materially hurts the rotation-heavy mid_weak window.
next_retry_requires: Do not keep sweeping nearby Commodity trend targets above 7 ATR.
A valid retry needs a state or event discriminator explaining when late/old continuation should be held without damaging mid_weak.
Do not use post-target continuation audits alone as production evidence; require production-path replay.
notes: Alpha search, not measurement repair. Production strategy code was unchanged.
